task_completion.description: Open the One-Way ANOVA analysis dialog in Jamovi
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'One-Way ANOVA' analysis setup panel is visible on the left side of the Jamovi interface.

task_completion.description: Assign 'libido' as the Dependent Variable and 'dose' as the Grouping Variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the One-Way ANOVA setup panel, 'libido' is placed in the 'Dependent Variables' box and 'dose' is placed in the 'Grouping Variable' box.

task_completion.description: Select the option to assume equal variances (Fisher's)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Variances' section in the setup panel, the 'Assume equal (Fisher's)' checkbox is checked.
Under the 'Post Hoc Tests' section in the setup panel, the 'Tukey' checkbox is checked.
Under the 'Additional Statistics' or similar section in the setup panel, the 'Descriptives table' checkbox is checked.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct One-Way ANOVA results are displayed in the output panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The right-hand results panel displays the One-Way ANOVA table (F=2.416, p=0.108), the Post Hoc Comparisons table (Tukey), and the Group Descriptives table (means: 3.22, 4.88, 4.85).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Jamovi graphical user interface
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Jamovi application window with menus and dialogs being used, rather than terminal commands or external scripts running R/Python.

integrity.description: Results are generated by Jamovi and not manually typed
integrity.visual_evidence: The statistical results appear in the standard Jamovi right-hand output panel with native table formatting, not in a text editor or separate document.

integrity.description: The correct Viagra.csv dataset is being used
integrity.visual_evidence: The data spreadsheet view in Jamovi shows the columns 'dose', 'libido', and 'partnerLibido'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct One-Way ANOVA (Fisher's) results for 'libido' by 'dose' yield an F-statistic of 2.416 and a p-value of 0.108. The Group Descriptives table should show means of 3.22 (Placebo/dose=1), 4.88 (Low dose/dose=2), and 4.85 (High dose/dose=3). Tukey post-hoc comparisons should show no significant differences (all p-values > 0.05).
